ground_truth.title: The chaos begins: Building my Home Assistant smart home from scratch
ground_truth.author: Franck Nijhof
ground_truth.main_content: We got the keys 48 hours ago. The house is full of boxes, our stuff is everywhere, and I have absolutely no idea how half the smart home equipment already installed actually works. This is the first video in my new series where I'm documenting the entire journey of building out a Home Assistant smart home from scratch. No polished "here's my finished setup" content. Just the real process: the decisions, the discoveries, the mystery devices, and every spectacular failure along the way. The previous owner left behind some interesting gear: a full Philips Hue outdoor lighting setup in the garden with a single remote controlling everything, 40 solar panels on the roof with a SolarEdge inverter I need to claim ownership of, and a jacuzzi with a Gecko control system that I'm determined to monitor through Home Assistant. I found two thermostats in the living room controlling something. The floor heating has a wireless Watts remote that I have no idea which room it's assigned to. There's a heat pump, multiple air conditioning units with missing remotes, and a mysterious "Hunter HC" box in the electrical closet with a Bluetooth logo. The electrician is coming Friday because the fusebox area is a huge mess. I'm tackling this by walking around with a camera, pointing at things, and saying "I need to figure out what this does". CES is coming up in January, so I'll be bringing back inspiration and ideas. But for now, it's time to unpack, set up my Wi-Fi network, and start figuring out how all these systems actually work.
with: We got the keys 48 hours ago. The house is full of boxes, our stuff is everywhere, and I have absolutely no idea how half the smart home equipment already installed actually works.

There's a full Philips Hue outdoor lighting setup in the garden (with a single remote controlling everything), 40 solar panels on the roof with a SolarEdge inverter I need to claim ownership of, and a jacuzzi with a Gecko control system that I'm determined to monitor through Home Assistant.
I found two thermostats in the living room controlling... something. The floor heating has a wireless Watts remote that I have no idea which room it's assigned to.

But here's where it gets fun: I found two thermostats in the living room controlling... something. The floor heating has a wireless Watts remote that I have no idea which room it's assigned to. There's a heat pump, multiple air conditioning units with missing remotes, and a mysterious "Hunter HC" box in the electrical closet and one that has a Bluetooth logo.
What would you tackle first in this setup? Drop your thoughts in the YouTube comments. I'm genuinely curious what priorities you'd set with this much smart home potential and this many unknowns.
